  B系列的其他投资者包括新的投资者贝塞尔风险伙伴，现有的投资者正和其他人。公司成立于2018年，该公司已筹集超过4500万美元。
 Prefect的DataFlow Automation Platform允许公司在运行应用程序时简化其工作流程和计划。大多数应用程序都有一个预设的计划来运行 - 有时与公司使用它的内容不相关 - 而且在运行时可以帮助定制，并确保它在其所有数据中进行自定义。
 由于现代数据堆栈为公司爆炸，创始人兼首席执行官Jeremiah Lowin表示，人们可以将平台视为牙签，在夹层中握住它。
   洛地表示，自去年年初在市场亮相以来，其商业产品观察到其商业产品增长了130％至季度。虽然公司没有发布财务，但洛格表示，该公司拥有几个垂直的“几百”客户，特别是金融服务和医疗保健。
 Lowin表示，该公司将使用新鲜资本加入其25人的25人队伍及其产品。 “我们在这个增长轨迹上，我们希望与每天生活的增长公司合作，”他说。 “这些公司是我们正在经历的增长类型的专家。”
 该公司希望在一点竞争中扩展。 Lowin除了试图建立自己的解决方案的公司之外，开源工作流程管理平台Apache气流很可能是强大的竞争对手。值得注意的是，Lowin表示，他在创建职业之前在对冲基金工作时使用了Apache气流。 
贝塞姆勒的Ethan Kurzweil表示，在看市场需求后，在屈服中投资是一个明显的决定。  “历史上，大型科技公司已经在内部建造了这种类型的软件，导致数据团队的实施和用户体验造成巨大挫折感，”他说。  KURZWEIL表示，他在新兴部门看到了一个伟大的未来：“我们认为他们在鉴于他们对用户，客户，合作伙伴和其他主要参与者的影响方面继续他们的道路时，他们将继续前进。 新数据基础架构堆栈。  “追求者将永远来，但是强度让所有元素成为一个持久的业务，以造成自己的道路，”他说。  及时了解最近的资金回合，收购和更多  每天嘎吱嘎吱。
note_en:
Washington, D.C.-based  Prefect closed a $32 million Series B led by  Tiger Global Management to help eliminate data flow problems for companies’ data teams.
  Other investors in the Series B included new investor  Bessemer Venture Partners, existing investor  Positive Sum and others. Founded in 2018, the company has raised more than $45 million to date.
 Prefect’s dataflow automation platform allows companies to streamline their workflows and schedule when applications run. Most applications have a preset schedule to run — which at times does not correlate with what a company is using it for — and Prefect can help customize when it runs and make sure it is taking in all the data it should.
 As the modern data stack explodes for companies, founder and CEO  Jeremiah Lowin said one can think of the platform as the toothpick in a sandwich that holds it all together.
   Prefect has watched its commercial product grow 130 percent quarter to quarter since debuting in the market early last year, Lowin said. While the company does not release financials, Lowin said the company has a “few hundred” customers in several verticals, especially financial services and health care.
 The company will use the fresh capital to add to both its 25-person team and its product, Lowin said. “We are on this growth trajectory and we wanted to partner with growth firms that live this every day,” he said. “These firms are experts in the type of growth we are experiencing.”
 The company is looking to expand in a market with little competition. Lowin said aside from companies trying to build their own solution, open-source workflow management platform Apache Airflow is likely Prefect’s biggest competitor. Notably, Lowin said he used Apache Airflow while working at a hedge fund before founding Prefect.
 Ethan Kurzweil at Bessemer said making an investment in Prefect was an obvious decision after looking at the need in the market.
 “Historically, large tech companies have built out this type of software in-house, resulting in massive frustration in implementation and user experience for data teams,” he said.
 Kurzweil said he sees a big future for Prefect in the emerging sector: “We believe there is a huge outcome ahead should they continue their path as an independent company given the impact they are having on users, customers, partners and other major players in the new data infrastructure stack.
 “Suitors will always come, but Prefect has all of the elements to be an enduring business that forges its own path,” he said.

 “法国银行确信批发中央银行数字货币的潜在利益，为金融交易提供最大的安全性和效率，”Goulard表示。
 在将瑞士和法国分开的山脉之后，两个中央银行之间的合作已被称为汝拉。 
它将涉及欧元批发CBDC对瑞士法郎批发CBDC的交换，通过付款与支付结算机制。这些交易将在基于法国和瑞士的银行之间结算。
 该技术意味着支付几乎是即时瞬间，而中央银行有效地必须在他们经历之前进行数字批准。
 “为了使CBDC成为真实的，他们需要能够跨越边界和保留主权的方式使用，”R3坚定的联合创始人托德·麦克唐纳·麦克唐纳（Todd）表示。
   中央银行表示，该项目是“探索性”，并不表明他们的数字货币将完全介绍。
 该计划将在未来几个月运行，也是去年在瑞士在瑞士推出的最新部分Helvetia实验，以使用批发CBDC使用销量资产。
  在中国和阿拉伯联合酋长国的中央银行加入了一个跨国数字货币项目之后，涉足涉足M-Bridge的跨境数字货币项目，伙伴关系是在香港的BIS的创新臂。
 SNB理事会成员Andrea Maechler表示，她的银行正在参与其中，因为“这对央行至关重要的技术发展。”批发数字货币，通常仅限于将央行持有账户的金融机构，与公众提供的零售CBDC不同。 
它们被视为中央银行中最受欢迎的建议，因为有可能使现有的批发金融系统更快，更便宜，更安全。  SENB对Facebook的DIEM项目等数字货币持怀疑态度，以前称为天秤座，称他们可能会破坏其进行货币政策的能力。

ZURICH/LONDON (Reuters) - The Swiss National Bank and the Bank of France will trial Europe’s first cross-border central bank digital currency payments, the banks said on Thursday, a move that will be a first for two of the world’s top currencies.
  The experiment will focus on the bank-to-bank ‘wholesale’ lending market rather than everyday public transactions but will be the first time a digital euro and Swiss franc have been fully tested.
 Switzerland’s UBS, Credit Suisse and France’s Natixis are involved in the project, along with Swiss bourse operator SIX Digital Exchange, fintech firm R3 and the Innovation Hub at the Bank for International Settlements.
 The eurozone is adapting to a “strong trend” towards the digitalisation of payments,” said Bank of France Deputy Governor Sylvie Goulard.
  It is the Bank of France’s latest CBDC pilot. In April, it ‘settled’ - or made payments on - digital bonds issued by the European Investment Bank using blockchain technology.
 “The Bank of France is convinced of the potential benefits of wholesale central bank digital currency to provide maximum security and efficiency in financial transactions,” Goulard said.
 The collaboration between the two central banks has been dubbed Jura, after the mountain range which separates Switzerland and France.
  It will involve the exchange of a euro wholesale CBDC against a Swiss franc wholesale CBDC through a payment versus payment settlement mechanism. These transactions will be settled between banks based in France and Switzerland.
 The technology means payments will be almost instant while both central banks effectively have to digitally approve them before they can go through.
 “In order for CBDCs to be real they need to be able to be used across borders and in a way that preserves sovereignty,” said Todd McDonald, co-founder of the R3 firm behind the technology.
   The central banks said the project was “exploratory” and was not an indication that their digital currencies would be fully introduced.
 The scheme, which will run for the next few months, is also the latest part of the Project Helvetia experiment launched in Switzerland last year into using tokenised assets with wholesale CBDC.
  It comes, too, after the central banks of China and United Arab Emirates joined a cross-border digital currency project called Multiple CBDC dubbed m-bridge in partnership’s with the BIS’ innovation arm based in Hong Kong.
 SNB governing board member Andrea Maechler said her bank was taking part because “it is essential for central banks to stay on top of technological developments.” Wholesale digital currencies, usually limited to financial institutions which hold accounts with a central bank, are different from retail CBDCs which are available to the general public.
 They are seen as the most popular proposal among central banks because of the potential to make existing wholesale financial systems faster, cheaper and safer.
 The SNB has been skeptical about digital currencies like Facebook’s Diem project, formerly known as Libra, saying they could undermine its ability to conduct monetary policy.
title: 瑞士中央银行和法国试验欧洲的第一个跨境数字货币支付，重点关注银行到银行批发贷款市场
title_en: Central banks of Switzerland and France trial Europe's first cross-border digital currency payments, focusing on the bank-to-bank wholesale lending market
